📂 GitHub Repository:
https://github.com/tahaahmad-max/Codealpha_tasks/tree/main/Task_3_Project%20Management%20Tool
Linkedin Post:
https://www.linkedin.com/posts/taha-ahmad-0a833337a_python-django-webdevelopment-activity-7482015787956895745-sEU3?utm_source=share&utm_medium=member_desktop&rcm=ACoAAF2qS8ABh8HHDdAMVC4i3ZV7MLxdbuTZxag
Building practical, real-world applications is one of the best ways to strengthen software development skills. For this project, I developed a Project Management Tool inspired by platforms like Trello and Asana using Django for the backend and HTML, CSS, and Vanilla JavaScript for the frontend.
The goal was to create a beginner-friendly application that demonstrates the core concepts of project management systems while keeping the code clean, well-structured, and easy to understand. This project also provided valuable experience in organizing larger Django applications and implementing features commonly found in collaborative productivity tools.
🛠️ Tech Stack

• Python

• Django

• HTML5

• CSS3

• Vanilla JavaScript

• SQLite
✨ Features
🔐 User Authentication
The application uses Django’s built-in authentication system to provide:

• User Registration

• User Login

• User Logout

• Password validation
Only authenticated users can access the project dashboard and collaborate on projects.
📊 Dashboard
Each user has a personalized dashboard where they can:

• View all their projects

• See project descriptions

• Check the number of tasks

• View project members

• Create new projects
📁 Project Management
Users can:

• Create new projects

• Edit project details

• Delete projects

• Add multiple members to projects

• View all project members
📋 Kanban Project Board
Each project includes a simple Kanban-style board with three task columns:

• To Do

• In Progress
Completed
Tasks are organized according to their status, making project tracking simple and intuitive.
✅ Task Management
Users can:

• Create tasks

• Edit tasks

• Delete tasks

• Assign tasks to project members

• Set due dates

• Define task priority (Low, Medium, High)

• Update task status
Task cards are color-coded based on priority for better visibility.
💬 Comments
Team members can collaborate by:

• Adding comments to tasks

• Viewing discussion history

• Seeing comment authors and timestamps
👤 User Profiles
Users can manage their profile information, including:

• Full name

• Username

• Email

• Optional profile picture

• Profile editing
🔔 Notifications
A simple notification system keeps users informed when:

• A new task is assigned to them

• Someone comments on their assigned task
Notifications refresh whenever the page reloads, providing a lightweight alternative to real-time updates.
⚙️ Django Admin Panel
The Django Admin interface allows administrators to manage:

• Users

• Projects

• Tasks

• Comments

• Notifications
🗄️ Database Design
The project is built around the following models:

• User (Django Authentication)

• Project

• ProjectMember

• Task

• Comment

• Notification
These models helped me better understand relationships between users, projects, tasks, and collaborative workflows.
📚 What I Learned
Building this project improved my understanding of:

• Django project architecture

• Authentication and authorization

• Models, Views, Templates, and URL routing

• CRUD operations

• Database relationships

• Kanban board implementation

• Task assignment workflows

• Notification systems

• Team collaboration features

• Responsive frontend development

• Organizing scalable Django applications
This project also strengthened my problem-solving skills by combining multiple interconnected features into a single, fully functional web application.
🚀 Conclusion
Developing this Project Management Tool was an excellent learning experience that combined backend development, frontend design, authentication, database management, and collaborative workflows into one complete application.
There are several enhancements that could be added in future versions, such as real-time notifications using WebSockets, drag-and-drop task movement, file attachments, project analytics, calendar integration, email notifications, and activity logs.
If you’re learning Django and want to build something beyond basic CRUD applications, a project like this is a great way to gain practical experience with real-world software development concepts.
Feel free to explore the project on GitHub, and I’d love to hear your feedback or suggestions. Happy coding!
